# GitAll — Full Reference

> Unified contribution heatmap viewer for GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Gitea/Forgejo

**URL:** https://gitall.app
**Built by:** Toastbyte Studios (https://toastbyte.studio)

---

## Overview

GitAll is a free, open-source developer tool that aggregates contribution graphs from
all major git platforms into one unified heatmap. Enter usernames for any supported
platform and instantly see combined git activity — no account or login required.

---

## Supported Platforms

- **GitHub** — github.com (the world's largest code hosting platform)
- **GitLab** — gitlab.com (self-hosted and cloud git repositories)
- **Bitbucket** — bitbucket.org (Atlassian's git collaboration platform)
- **Gitea** — gitea.com and any self-hosted Gitea instance
- **Forgejo** — any self-hosted Forgejo instance (e.g. Codeberg, which is the default)

---

## Features

- View contribution graphs from GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Gitea/Forgejo side by side
- Merge contributions from all platforms into a single integrated cross-platform heatmap
- Multi-user comparison — compare contribution activity across multiple developers
- Custom time ranges — select any date range for contribution analysis
- Sign in with GitHub OAuth to display your GitHub identity and include your own private GitHub contributions
- No account required for public contribution data — anonymous lookup supported
- Free to use, no paid tiers
- Custom Gitea/Forgejo instance URL support — point to any self-hosted instance
- Embeddable SVG heatmap — add your contribution graph to GitHub READMEs, portfolios, and personal sites

---

## How It Works

1. Enter your username(s) for each supported platform in the search form
2. GitAll fetches public contribution data directly from each platform's API
3. View contributions side by side or as a single integrated cross-platform heatmap

---

## API Endpoints

GitAll proxies platform APIs to avoid CORS restrictions and cache responses:

- `GET /api/github?username=<user>` — GitHub contribution data
- `GET /api/gitlab?username=<user>` — GitLab contribution data
- `GET /api/bitbucket?username=<user>` — Bitbucket contribution data
- `GET /api/gitea?username=<user>&instanceUrl=<url>` — Gitea/Forgejo contribution data

---

## Embeddable Heatmap

GitAll generates embeddable SVG contribution heatmaps that can be used in GitHub READMEs, portfolio sites, and anywhere that supports image embeds.

### Embed endpoint

```
GET /embed/<github_username>.svg
GET /embed/<github_username>.svg?github=<user>&gitlab=<user>&bitbucket=<user>&gitea=<user>&instance=<url>
```

**Query parameters:**
- `github` — GitHub username (overrides the path username)
- `gitlab` — GitLab username
- `bitbucket` — Bitbucket workspace slug
- `gitea` — Gitea/Forgejo username
- `instance` — Gitea/Forgejo instance URL (default: codeberg.org)
- `theme` — `dark` (default) or `light`

**Markdown snippet (GitHub README):**
```markdown
![GitAll contributions](https://gitall.app/embed/octocat.svg)
```

**HTML snippet:**
```html
<img src="https://gitall.app/embed/octocat.svg" alt="Contribution heatmap" />
```

The embed is cached at the Cloudflare edge for 24 hours and includes a subtle "Powered by GitAll" watermark.

---

## Use Cases

- Developers who contribute across multiple git platforms want a single unified view
- Recruiters and engineering managers assessing developer activity across platforms
- Open-source contributors active on both GitHub and GitLab/Gitea/Codeberg
- Anyone who wants to compare their contribution history across platforms
- Teams comparing developer activity for multiple contributors side by side

---

## Technical Details

- Built with Next.js 15 + React 19, deployed on Cloudflare Workers via OpenNext
- Contribution data is fetched directly from platform APIs; no data is stored server-side
- Response caching via in-memory cache with 15-minute TTL and stale-while-revalidate
- Theme: supports light/dark mode via CSS custom properties and `data-theme` attribute

---

## FAQ

**Q: Does GitAll support Bitbucket?**
A: Yes. Bitbucket is fully supported. Enter your Bitbucket username to see your contribution data.

**Q: Does GitAll support Gitea or Forgejo?**
A: Yes. GitAll supports Gitea and Forgejo, including Codeberg. You can also enter a custom instance URL for any self-hosted Gitea or Forgejo server.

**Q: Is GitAll free?**
A: Yes, GitAll is completely free to use.

**Q: Do I need an account?**
A: No. Public contribution data is fetched anonymously — no sign-up, no OAuth required. Sign in with GitHub only to display your GitHub identity and include your own private GitHub contributions in the heatmap.

**Q: How many platforms does GitAll support?**
A: GitAll supports 4 major platforms: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Gitea/Forgejo (including Codeberg).

---

## Competitive Advantages

- Supports 4+ platforms (most competitors only support GitHub + GitLab)
- Multi-user comparison across all platforms
- Custom time ranges for contribution analysis
- OAuth integration to display GitHub identity and include private GitHub contributions for the signed-in user
- Self-hosted Gitea/Forgejo instance support
